title: MetaFFI Entity Path Specifications
description: Defines the entity_path structure expected by each language runtime plugin. IDL compilers use this specification to generate correct entity_path structures.

optional_flags.varargs: Set to true if function accepts *args
optional_flags.named_args: Set to true if function accepts **kwargs

optional_flags.instance_required: Set to true for instance methods, false for static/class methods
optional_flags.varargs: Set to true if method accepts *args
optional_flags.named_args: Set to true if method accepts **kwargs

optional_flags.varargs: Set to true if constructor accepts *args
optional_flags.named_args: Set to true if constructor accepts **kwargs

optional_flags.instance_required: Set to true for instance fields, false for class/static fields
Set to true for instance fields, false for class/static fields

cpp.description: C/C++ runtime plugin entity_path structure. Symbol names are carried as-is (already-mangled); the runtime_manager never mangles or demangles.

free_function.description: C or C++ free function, or a C++ static method. Symbol must be the already-mangled name (use the plain name for extern "C" / pure-C functions).

required_keys.callable: Symbol name exactly as it appears in the shared library's export table (e.g. 'add', '_ZN3Foo6doWorkEi', '?doWork@Foo@@QAEHH@Z')

optional_keys.namespace: C++ namespace path (e.g. 'myns', 'outer::inner'). Used for IDL grouping only — namespace is already encoded in the mangled callable symbol.

instance_method.description: C++ instance method. The caller passes the object pointer (this*) as the first argument when invoking the function.

required_keys.callable: Mangled symbol name of the instance method

optional_keys.namespace: C++ namespace path. Used for IDL grouping — namespace is already encoded in the mangled symbol.

constructor.description: C++ constructor. The plugin allocates class_size bytes, then calls the constructor function pointer with the memory as this*.

required_keys.callable: Mangled symbol name of the constructor
required_keys.class_size: sizeof(class) as a decimal integer — number of bytes to allocate

optional_keys.namespace: C++ namespace path. Used for IDL grouping only.

destructor.description: C++ destructor. destroy() calls the destructor function pointer with the instance pointer, then frees the memory.

required_keys.callable: Mangled symbol name of the destructor

optional_keys.namespace: C++ namespace path. Used for IDL grouping only.

field_getter.description: C++ field getter using byte offset arithmetic. Returns a pointer to the field inside the instance (no symbol lookup).

required_keys.field: Logical field name (used for diagnostics and IDL grouping)
required_keys.field_offset: Byte offset of the field within the class, as a decimal integer

required_flags.instance_required: Always true — field belongs to an instance

optional_keys.namespace: C++ namespace path. Used for IDL grouping only.

field_setter.description: C++ field setter using byte offset and memcpy. No symbol lookup is performed.

required_keys.field_offset: Byte offset of the field within the class, as a decimal integer
required_keys.field_size: Size of the field in bytes, as a decimal integer

required_flags.instance_required: Always true — field belongs to an instance

optional_keys.namespace: C++ namespace path. Used for IDL grouping only.

global_getter.description: Read a C/C++ global variable. Returns a void* pointing directly into the module's memory for the variable (zero-copy).

required_keys.global: Symbol name of the global variable (e.g. 'g_counter')

optional_keys.namespace: C++ namespace path. Used for IDL grouping — namespace is already encoded in the mangled symbol.

global_setter.description: Write a C/C++ global variable by memcpy-ing caller-supplied bytes into the variable's memory.

required_keys.global: Symbol name of the global variable (e.g. 'g_counter')

optional_keys.namespace: C++ namespace path. Used for IDL grouping — namespace is already encoded in the mangled symbol.

notes.name_mangling: The IDL compiler (sdk/idl_compiler/cpp/) produces mangled names using libclang. The runtime_manager never mangles or demangles symbols.
notes.abi_verification: The plugin detects the C++ ABI of each loaded module (MSVC vs Itanium) and hard-fails on a mismatch. Pure-C modules (no C++ runtime dependency) are accepted unconditionally.
notes.namespace_key: The optional namespace= key is stored for IDL grouping purposes but does not affect symbol lookup. The mangled callable/global symbol already encodes namespace information.

optional_flags.instance_required: Set to true for instance methods, false for static methods
Set to true for instance fields, false for static fields
Set to true for instance fields, false for static fields
